name: dentate gyrus granule cell
morphology_summary: Compact cell bodies in stratum granulosum; thin unmyelinated axons (mossy fibers) projecting into stratum lucidum of CA3. Approximately one million GCs in rat dentate gyrus. Mossy fiber terminals co-express VGLUT1, VGLUT2, and VGAT. Dendritic spines receive first glutamatergic input ~2 weeks after birth.
ephys_summary: Lower firing threshold than typical cortical neurons; express AMPA and NMDA receptors at dendritic spines. Mossy fiber axons can produce mixed GABAergic and glutamatergic monosynaptic currents in CA3 principal cells (juvenile animals). GluR1 expression begins profoundly ~3 weeks after cell division.
subtypes_noted: 85% generated postnatally; adult-born (immature) granule cells represent a distinct functional subpopulation with delayed maturation. Dorsal vs. ventral heterogeneity implied.

name: hilar mossy cell
morphology_summary: Large somata with thick bushy proximal dendrites encrusted with thorny excrescences (complex spines) receiving mossy fiber input from granule cells. Dendrites mainly in the hilus. Axon makes local hilar collaterals (~25% of axon) and extensive projections to the inner molecular layer across multiple septotemporal levels (>60% of axon). Commissural projection to contralateral DG. Dorsal mossy cells additionally project to the middle molecular layer.
ephys_summary: Regular-spiking firing pattern confirmed in hilar cells with thorny excrescences. Produce unitary EPSPs in granule cells, confirming glutamatergic output. Reelin-positive mossy cells in the dentate hilus are predominantly glutamatergic.
subtypes_noted: Dorsal vs. ventral mossy cells differ in axonal projection patterns: dorsal MCs project to both IML and MML; ventral MC long-range axons terminate in IML only. Labeled with Drd2-Cre and Crlr-Cre mouse lines.

name: CA3 pyramidal cell
morphology_summary: Cell bodies in stratum pyramidale of CA3; receive mossy fiber input from granule cells in stratum lucidum. Form Schaffer collateral projections to CA1. GluA1 immunoreactivity diffuse in str. radiatum and oriens with localization to pyramidal cell bodies in str. pyramidale. Form gap junctions (mixed electrical-chemical glutamatergic synapses via Cx36).
ephys_summary: Fast excitatory synaptic transmission mediated by AMPA receptors; NMDA receptors serve slower modulatory role. CA3 pyramidal neurons form mixed glutamatergic synapses with gap junctions. Express mGluR1 at high levels. AMPA, kainate, and NMDA receptors broadly co-localized at individual spines.
subtypes_noted: Pioneer early-generated CA3 glutamatergic neurons (Marissal et al., 2012) form a morpho-functionally distinct subpopulation with distinct features persisting from juvenile to adult hippocampus.

name: CA1 pyramidal cell
negative_markers_mentioned: D1R (dorsal CA1 pyramidal cells are D1R-negative)
morphology_summary: Cell bodies in stratum pyramidale of CA1; receive Schaffer collateral input from CA3 and project to subiculum and entorhinal cortex. High Np65 (neuroplastin-65) expression at cell body membranes and dendrites. GluA1 shows strong dense immunoreactivity in str. oriens and radiatum; GluA2 localized to cell bodies in str. pyramidale with diffuse staining in oriens and radiatum.
ephys_summary: Fast excitatory transmission via AMPA receptors; NMDA receptors serve modulatory role. Np65 regulates the number and function of excitatory synapses in CA1. D1R is not expressed by CA1 pyramidal cells in dorsal hippocampus (expressed only on interneurons there).
subtypes_noted: Ventral CA1 pyramidal neurons express D1/D2 dopamine receptors (45.1% of D1/D2 cells in vHipp are glutamatergic), likely corresponding to projection-specific vCA1/vSubiculum populations. Np65-positive glutamatergic neurons include CA1, CA2-3, and subiculum pyramidal neurons.

name: medial septal / diagonal band of Broca glutamatergic neuron
anatomy_mentioned: hippocampal stratum oriens (projection target near alveus)
morphology_summary: Soma in medial septum/diagonal band of Broca; project (~23% of MS projections) to hippocampal interneurons with somata in stratum oriens near the alveus. Express VGluT1 and/or VGluT2; lack ChAT and GAD.
ephys_summary: Highly heterogeneous firing: four subtypes — (1) fast-spiking with little AP accommodation, sometimes spontaneous, some with pronounced Ih sag; (2) cluster-firing with subthreshold membrane oscillations, strong AP accommodation, little/no sag (unique to glutamatergic MS neurons); (3) burst-firing with little/no sag; (4) slow-firing with accommodating APs. VGluT2 neurons fire clusters at 8-14 Hz (half of glutamatergic neurons). Can spontaneously fire rhythmically at theta frequencies. Activity increases before locomotion onset and during running.
subtypes_noted: Four electrophysiologically distinct VGluT2+ subgroups (fast-spiking, cluster-firing, burst-firing, slow-firing). Cluster firing is a unique electrophysiological marker for glutamatergic (not GABAergic) MS-DBB neurons.

name: hippocampal Cajal-Retzius cell
morphology_summary: Located in stratum lacunosum-moleculare (SLM) and outer molecular layer (OML). Can be subdivided into: (1) local-projecting CR cells with somatodendritic and axonal domains restricted to one layer, functioning as glutamatergic interneurons; (2) local-projecting CR cells crossing the hippocampal fissure (SLM to OML); (3) long-range projecting CR cells targeting distant subregions. Biased toward targeting dendritic shafts over spines. Secrete reelin glycoprotein. More abundant in adult rats than previously assumed.
ephys_summary: Produce large-amplitude glutamatergic unitary postsynaptic potentials on GABAergic interneurons. Optogenetically-evoked EPSCs in interneurons show faster kinetics than in pyramidal cells. Responses blocked by TTX; rescued by 4-AP in interneurons, indicating monosynaptic connection to interneurons. Both AMPA- and NMDA-type glutamate receptors are involved in CR cell output. Provide feedforward GABAergic input to pyramidal cells via interneuron activation.
subtypes_noted: Mixed population: some CR cells are GABAergic, others glutamatergic; the two subtypes interact. Glutamatergic CR cells express calretinin and reelin and are immunopositive for VGluT3 (pioneer cells). Three axonal subtypes: layer-restricted local, cross-fissure local, and long-range projecting.

name: CA1 radiatum giant cell
morphology_summary: Excitatory projection neuron located in CA1 stratum radiatum; newly discovered at the time of citation (Kirson et al., 2000). Distinct from pyramidal cells; treated as a specialized CA1 glutamatergic projection neuron.
ephys_summary: Glutamatergic synaptic activation fires an NMDA receptor-dependent burst of action potentials even after blocking non-NMDA receptors — a unique property distinguishing RGCs from typical pyramidal cells. Enhanced synaptic excitability due to unique properties of synaptic NMDA receptors.
subtypes_noted: Not discussed beyond single citation; treated as a single specialized type.
